verification: Second delivery copy of the config-resolution-closure mission as loose files; mission already superseded by merged PR #3079 (ResolvedRuntimeConfig) per prior r01 adjudication. Retained for provenance only.

reassessments.trigger: identical-SHA duplicate re-download landed at /realm/tmp/gpt-pro-intake-0719/config-*.md/.diff on 2026-07-19; per custody policy (results/README.md: 'Duplicated browser downloads with identical SHA-256 values are deliberately not copied twice') no new package revision or raw/extracted copy was created -- sha256 of PATCH.diff/HANDOFF.md/EVIDENCE.md/TESTS.md all match this r01 record exactly (diff -q reports no differences).
reassessments.finding: PR #3079 (merged 2026-07-18T04:23Z, 'feat(config): close the 5-layer resolution gap with ResolvedRuntimeConfig') already shipped ResolvedRuntimeConfig/single-resolver/archive_root-fix from an earlier revision of this exact GPT-Pro packet family -- its own PR body states it was 'recovered as config-closure-current.diff from a GPT Pro packet' (a 1717-line variant is preserved at misc-01/r01/raw/config-closure-current (3).diff, distinct sha 9010ddf7e..., vs. this delivery's fuller 5377-line/73-file diff). Live-checked polylogue/config.py:1514 _merge_toml/_deep_merge_table: nested health tables (health_convergence_debt, health_cursor_lag) already deep-merge on master, closing the polylogue-cxlk bug shape this delivery also targets. There is no viable rebase target for this patch against the current config API: #3079 shipped a differently-shaped ResolvedRuntimeConfig with different composition-root injection points and no shared ancestor with this delivery's own implementation, so applying or reconciling this patch's hunks is not the right motion -- any further work must be RE-DERIVED against the already-landed API, not revived from this diff. bd polylogue-fd2s and polylogue-9gh1 (epic) still read status=open (epic_closed_children=0) in bd -- read as bookkeeping lag behind #3079's landing, not evidence the architecture is unfixed; flagged for a separate bd housekeeping pass, out of this triage's scope. GENUINE RESIDUAL, TRACKED DURABLY: bd polylogue-uu8r (open, P2) targets ~31 direct-os.environ call sites outside config.py; spot-checked two files this delivery's own bypass-caller table names -- polylogue/daemon/backup.py:695 (POLYLOGUE_BACKUP_VERIFY_TMPDIR) and polylogue/pipeline/services/archive_ingest.py:48 (POLYLOGUE_INGEST_COMMIT_BATCH_MESSAGES) -- both still read os.environ.get() directly on current master, i.e. NOT migrated by #3079. This delivery's migration for those two files plus ~5-6 more (sources/live/batch_support.py, sources/parsers/antigravity.py, sources/hooks.py, ui/theme.py, readiness/__init__.py, logging.py) is real, uncaptured value, but is small-to-medium re-derivation work against the current config API, not a portable hunk from this patch. The residual is tracked as polylogue-uu8r directly, not as a pending state on this package.
reassessments.reverification: git apply --check re-run against the stated snapshot 536a53efac0cbe4a2473ad379e4db49ef3fce74d: still passes cleanly. Re-run against origin/master at both e963e87f5 and (after rebase) 1c0f4eccd: fails identically both times on all 21 production files that touch the config/services/paths core (polylogue/config.py, services.py, paths/_roots.py, cli/click_app.py, cli/shared/types.py, daemon/cli.py, mcp/server.py, storage/embeddings/*, etc.) plus 4 of its core test modules. #3175 (feat(cli): converge first-run onboarding, landed between the two checks) touches cli/click_app.py, cli/commands/dashboard.py, and cli/commands/tutorial.py -- all three appear in this delivery's file list -- adding incremental drift on peripheral CLI files, but does not touch config.py/services.py/paths/_roots.py, so it does not change the core-architecture supersession finding.
